circuit:Abu Dhabi.title: Yas Marina Circuit
circuit:Abu Dhabi.summary: The Yas Marina Circuit, designed by Hermann Tilke, is a 5. 281 km track located on Yas Island in Abu Dhabi. Since 2009, it has served as the venue for Formula One's season-concluding Abu Dhabi Grand Prix, making it the second Middle Eastern Formula One circuit after Bahrain.

circuit:Australia.title: Albert Park Circuit
circuit:Australia.summary: A street circuit encircling Albert Park Lake in Melbourne's Albert Park suburb hosts the Formula One Australian Grand Prix each year, along with various supporting motorsport categories. The track holds FIA Grade 1 certification status.

circuit:Austria.title: Red Bull Ring
circuit:Austria.summary: Located in Spielberg, Austria, this 4. 326 km circuit was originally established as the Österreichring and served as the venue for the Austrian Grand Prix annually from 1970 through 1987. Following a redesign and renaming, the track returned to hosting the Grand Prix from 1997 to 2003.

circuit:Azerbaijan.title: Baku City Circuit
circuit:Azerbaijan.summary: Baku City Circuit is a street track in Azerbaijan's capital, stretching 6. 003 km to rank among Formula One's longest venues. The circuit first hosted a grand prix in 2016 before becoming the permanent home of the Azerbaijan Grand Prix from 2017 onward.

circuit:Bahrain.title: Bahrain International Circuit
circuit:Bahrain.summary: The Bahrain International Circuit, a 5. 412 km FIA Grade 1 facility opened in 2004, hosts the annual Formula One Bahrain Grand Prix, which marked the championship's inaugural Middle Eastern race that year. Beyond Formula One, the venue accommodates various motorsport events including GP2 racing, endurance competitions, and previously featured touring car racing between 2006 and 2010.

circuit:Barcelona.title: Circuit de Barcelona-Catalunya
circuit:Barcelona.summary: Located in Montmeló near Barcelona, Spain, the Circuit de Barcelona-Catalunya spans 4. 657 kilometers and features a balanced design combining extended straights with diverse corner configurations, making it suitable for testing all aspects of vehicle performance. The venue holds an FIA Grade 1 license and accommodates approximately 140,700 spectators.

circuit:Belgium.title: Circuit de Spa-Francorchamps
circuit:Belgium.summary: Located near Spa in Belgium's Wallonia region, this 7. 004 km circuit hosts the Belgian Grand Prix and boasts a rich Formula One heritage dating back to 1950. The venue has maintained an almost continuous presence on the F1 calendar since 1985, with only two exceptions in 2003 and 2006.

circuit:Canada.title: Circuit Gilles Villeneuve
circuit:Canada.summary: Located on Notre Dame Island in Montreal, Quebec, the Circuit Gilles Villeneuve spans 4. 361 kilometers and serves as the permanent home of the Canadian Grand Prix in Formula One. Beyond its role in international single-seater racing, the track has accommodated various prestigious motorsport competitions, including world sportscar championships and North American racing series.

circuit:China.title: Shanghai International Circuit
circuit:China.summary: The Shanghai International Circuit is a motorsport facility located in Shanghai's Jiading District that primarily serves as the host venue for the Chinese Grand Prix, which ran annually from 2004 to 2019 before resuming in 2024. In addition to the Formula One event, the track began hosting the Shanghai ePrix starting in 2024.

circuit:Great Britain.title: Silverstone Circuit
circuit:Great Britain.summary: Located near villages in Northamptonshire, England, Silverstone hosted the inaugural World Championship race in 1950 and has served as the primary venue for the British Grand Prix since 1987, after sharing hosting duties with two other circuits for over three decades. The track continues to be a significant motorsport facility, accommodating multiple racing series including motorcycle competitions.

circuit:Hungary.title: Hungaroring
circuit:Hungary.summary: The Hungaroring is a 4. 381 km circuit located near Budapest, Hungary, that hosts the Formula One Hungarian Grand Prix. Built in just eight months starting in late 1985, it became the venue for the first Formula One race held in Eastern Europe when it opened in March 1986.

circuit:Italy.title: Autodromo Nazionale Monza
circuit:Italy.summary: Autodromo Nazionale Monza is a 5. 793 km Italian racing circuit constructed in 1922 near Milan, ranking as mainland Europe's oldest purpose-built track and the third globally after Brooklands and Indianapolis. It has continuously hosted the Italian Grand Prix since 1949, with only a 1980 interruption for renovations, earning the nickname "Temple of Speed" for its extended straights and demanding high-velocity turns.

circuit:Japan.title: Suzuka International Racing Course
circuit:Japan.summary: Suzuka Circuit is a 5. 807-kilometre Japanese motorsport venue operated by Honda Mobilityland in Mie Prefecture, featuring a capacity of 155,000 spectators. The track is primarily recognised as a premier Formula One and Super Formula racing destination.

circuit:Las Vegas.title: Las Vegas Strip Circuit
circuit:Las Vegas.summary: The Las Vegas Strip Circuit is a street course that navigates through Paradise, Nevada, running alongside famous hotels and casinos on Las Vegas Boulevard. The track winds through urban streets and incorporates iconic landmarks such as MSG Sphere, Caesars Palace, Bellagio, and Paris Las Vegas.

circuit:Mexico City.title: Autódromo Hermanos Rodríguez
circuit:Mexico City.summary: This 4. 304 km Mexico City circuit honours two deceased racing brothers, Ricardo and Pedro Rodríguez, after whom it was renamed in 1979 following their fatal accidents. The track has hosted the Formula One Mexican Grand Prix since 2015 on its current layout, resuming a tradition it previously maintained on an older configuration through 1992.

circuit:Miami.title: Miami International Autodrome
circuit:Miami.summary: The Miami International Autodrome is a temporary street circuit constructed around Hard Rock Stadium in Miami Gardens that measures 3. 363 miles with 19 corners, hosting qualifying speeds averaging 139 mph. Designed by specialist circuit architects, the venue became home to the Miami Grand Prix when it joined the Formula One calendar in 2022.

circuit:Monaco.title: Circuit de Monaco
circuit:Monaco.summary: The 3. 337 km circuit winds through the streets of Monaco's Monte Carlo district and La Condamine area, encircling the harbour of this principality. Though technically spanning multiple neighbourhoods, the track is universally known by the Monte Carlo name due to its primary location within that district.

circuit:Netherlands.title: Circuit Zandvoort
circuit:Netherlands.summary: Located in the Dutch coastal dunes near Amsterdam, the 4. 259 km Zandvoort circuit hosted its first Formula One race in seven decades when the Dutch Grand Prix returned to the calendar in 2021. The partnership between the venue and Formula One will conclude following the 2026 season.

circuit:Qatar.title: Lusail International Circuit
circuit:Qatar.summary: The Lusail International Circuit is a purpose-built racing venue situated north of Doha in Qatar, spanning 5. 419 kilometers with a prominent 1. 068-kilometer main straight.

circuit:São Paulo.title: Autódromo José Carlos Pace
circuit:São Paulo.summary: Interlagos is a 4. 309 km Brazilian racing circuit in São Paulo that opened in 1940 and was renamed in 1985 to commemorate Formula One driver José Carlos Pace, who perished in an aviation accident eight years earlier. The venue operates in a counterclockwise direction and features an associated kart track dedicated to fellow Brazilian racing legend Ayrton Senna.

circuit:Saudi Arabia.title: Jeddah Corniche Circuit
circuit:Saudi Arabia.summary: The Jeddah Corniche Circuit is a street circuit situated along the Red Sea waterfront in Saudi Arabia that has hosted the Formula One Saudi Arabian Grand Prix since its debut in 2021. The venue also accommodates international electric racing competitions, beginning with the Formula E event in 2025.

circuit:Singapore.title: Marina Bay Street Circuit
circuit:Singapore.summary: The Marina Bay Street Circuit winds through Singapore's Marina Bay district, incorporating sections of the Downtown Core and Kallang planning areas in an urban street racing configuration.

circuit:Spain.title: Circuit de Barcelona-Catalunya
circuit:Spain.summary: The Circuit de Barcelona-Catalunya is a 4. 657 km Spanish motorsport venue located in Montmeló near Barcelona, featuring a versatile layout with extended straights and diverse cornering challenges. The facility holds FIA Grade 1 accreditation and can accommodate approximately 140,700 spectators across its grandstands.

circuit:United States.title: Circuit of the Americas
circuit:United States.summary: Located in Austin, Texas, the Circuit of the Americas is a 3. 426-mile FIA Grade 1 facility that serves as the permanent home of the Formula One United States Grand Prix. The track also hosts major international motorsport events including MotoGP's Motorcycle Grand Prix of the Americas and various endurance racing championships, while previously accommodating IndyCar, rallycross, and sports car racing series.

driver:ALB.title: Alexander Albon
driver:ALB.summary: Alexander Albon is a Thai and British racing driver who represents Thailand in Formula One, currently competing for Williams.

driver:ALO.title: Fernando Alonso
driver:ALO.summary: Spanish driver Fernando Alonso competes for Aston Martin in Formula One, bringing two world championship titles from his 2005 and 2006 seasons with Renault and 32 career race victories. Beyond F1, Alonso has achieved remarkable success in endurance racing, including victories at Le Mans and the 2018-19 World Endurance Championship, making him the sole driver to have won both the F1 drivers' championship and the World Endurance drivers' championship.

driver:ANT.title: Andrea Kimi Antonelli
driver:ANT.summary: Italian driver Andrea Kimi Antonelli joined Mercedes in 2025 and has already secured five race victories in his Formula One career.

driver:BEA.title: Oliver Bearman
driver:BEA.summary: Oliver Bearman is a British Formula One driver currently racing for Haas, where he competes at the highest level of motorsport competition.

driver:BOR.title: Gabriel Bortoleto
driver:BOR.summary: Gabi Bortoleto, a Brazilian motorsport competitor, currently drives for Audi in Formula One competition.

driver:BOT.title: Valtteri Bottas
driver:BOT.summary: Finnish driver Valtteri Bottas has secured multiple runner-up finishes in the World Drivers' Championship, achieving this distinction during his tenure with Mercedes in both 2019 and 2020. Currently competing for Cadillac in Formula One, Bottas has accumulated 10 Grand Prix victories throughout his 13-season career.

driver:COL.title: Franco Colapinto
driver:COL.summary: Franco Alejandro Colapinto is an Argentine motorsport competitor who currently races for Alpine in Formula One.

driver:DOO.title: Jack Doohan (racing driver)
driver:DOO.summary: Jack Doohan is an Australian motorsport competitor who races in the European Le Mans Series' LMP2 category with Nielsen Racing while fulfilling reserve driver duties for Haas in Formula One. He has participated in seven Formula One Grand Prix events across the 2024 and 2025 seasons.

driver:GAS.title: Pierre Gasly
driver:GAS.summary: Pierre Gasly is a French Formula One driver currently competing for Alpine. He previously achieved his sole Grand Prix victory at the 2020 Italian Grand Prix while driving for AlphaTauri.

driver:HAD.title: Isack Hadjar
driver:HAD.summary: Isack Alexandre Hadjar, a driver holding French and Algerian nationality, races for Red Bull Racing in Formula One while competing under the French flag.

driver:HAM.title: Lewis Hamilton
driver:HAM.summary: Lewis Hamilton, a British driver, currently races for Ferrari in Formula One and has secured seven world championship titles, matching Michael Schumacher's record. He holds multiple all-time records including the most race victories, pole positions, and podium finishes throughout the sport's history.

driver:HUL.title: Nico Hülkenberg
driver:HUL.summary: Nicolas Hülkenberg, a German driver, currently races in Formula One for Audi. Beyond his single-seater commitments, he achieved success in endurance competition, notably claiming victory at the 24 Hours of Le Mans in 2015 while driving for Porsche.

driver:LAW.title: Liam Lawson
driver:LAW.summary: Liam Lawson, a New Zealand driver, currently races in Formula One for the Racing Bulls team.

driver:LEC.title: Charles Leclerc
driver:LEC.summary: Charles Leclerc, a Monégasque driver for Ferrari, finished second in the 2022 World Drivers' Championship and has accumulated nine race victories throughout his nine seasons in Formula One.

driver:MAG.title: Kevin Magnussen
driver:MAG.summary: Kevin Magnussen is a Danish driver currently competing in the World Endurance Championship with BMW through WRT, while also taking part in NASCAR's top series with Trackhouse Racing. He previously spent time in Formula One across two separate periods, racing from 2014-2020 and returning for the 2022-2024 seasons.

driver:NOR.title: Lando Norris
driver:NOR.summary: Lando Norris, a British driver, secured the 2025 Formula One World Drivers' Championship while racing for McLaren. Throughout his eight seasons in the sport, he has accumulated 11 Grand Prix victories.

driver:OCO.title: Esteban Ocon
driver:OCO.summary: Esteban Ocon is a French Formula One driver currently racing for Haas. The 2021 Hungarian Grand Prix victory with Alpine represents a significant achievement in his racing career.

driver:PER.title: Sergio Pérez
driver:PER.summary: Mexican driver Sergio Pérez currently races for Cadillac in Formula One, bringing extensive experience from 15 seasons in the sport. He finished as championship runner-up in 2023 while driving for Red Bull and has secured six race victories throughout his career.

driver:PIA.title: Oscar Piastri
driver:PIA.summary: Australian driver Oscar Piastri races for McLaren in Formula One, having claimed nine Grand Prix victories during his four seasons in the sport.

driver:RIC.title: Daniel Ricciardo
driver:RIC.summary: Australian driver Daniel Ricciardo competed in Formula One for 14 seasons between 2011 and 2024, securing eight race victories during his career. Known by the nickname "the Honey Badger," Ricciardo established himself as a competitive fixture in the sport across his time in the championship.

driver:RUS.title: George Russell (racing driver)
driver:RUS.summary: British driver George Russell competes for Mercedes in Formula One, having secured seven Grand Prix victories throughout his eight-year career in the sport.

driver:SAI.title: Carlos Sainz Jr.
driver:SAI.summary: Spanish driver Carlos Sainz competes for Williams in Formula One, where he has secured four race victories during his 12-season career in the sport.

driver:SAR.title: Logan Sargeant
driver:SAR.summary: Logan Sargeant is an American racing driver who most recently competed in Formula One during the 2023 and 2024 seasons. He has since transitioned to sports car racing, currently competing in the FIA World Endurance Championship's LMGT3 category for Proton Competition.

driver:STR.title: Lance Stroll
driver:STR.summary: Lance Stroll is a Canadian motorsport competitor who currently races for Aston Martin in Formula One.

driver:TSU.title: Yuki Tsunoda
driver:TSU.summary: Yuki Tsunoda is a Japanese driver who has held a reserve position with Red Bull Racing and Racing Bulls in Formula One, having competed in the sport from 2021 through 2025.

driver:VER.title: Max Verstappen
driver:VER.summary: A Dutch and Belgian driver competing under the Dutch flag, Verstappen has secured four consecutive world titles with Red Bull Racing between 2021 and 2024. Across 12 seasons in Formula One, he has accumulated 71 Grand Prix victories while remaining with the same team.

driver:ZHO.title: Zhou Guanyu
driver:ZHO.summary: Zhou Guanyu is China's sole Formula One competitor to date, having raced in the sport between 2022 and 2024. He currently holds a reserve driver position with Cadillac in Formula One.

team:Alpine.title: Alpine F1 Team
team:Alpine.summary: The Enstone-based team has competed as Alpine since 2021, continuing Renault's works operation following a rebranding from its previous Renault F1 identity to promote the French automaker's sports car division. Alpine's only victory came through Esteban Ocon at the 2021 Hungarian Grand Prix, and the team used Mercedes power units while maintaining a French racing licence until Renault's Formula One exit at the end of 2025.

team:Aston Martin.title: Aston Martin in Formula One
team:Aston Martin.summary: Aston Martin has made multiple entries into Formula One racing throughout its history, with the current incarnation operating under AMR GP Limited as an official works team.

team:Ferrari.title: Scuderia Ferrari
team:Ferrari.summary: Scuderia Ferrari, the racing arm of the Italian luxury automaker, holds the distinction of being Formula One's longest-running and most accomplished team, having participated in every world championship since the sport's inception in 1950. The squad, colloquially referred to as "the Prancing Horse" after its iconic emblem, remains a foundational institution in motorsport competition.

team:Haas F1 Team.title: Haas F1 Team
team:Haas F1 Team.summary: TGR Haas F1 Team is an American-licensed outfit founded by NASCAR team owner Gene Haas in 2014, entering Formula One competition in 2016. The team is currently led by principal Ayao Komatsu, who took over the role in 2024 following the departure of longtime leader Guenther Steiner.

team:Kick Sauber.title: Sauber Motorsport
team:Kick Sauber.summary: Established by Peter Sauber in 1970, this Swiss engineering outfit built sports cars before entering Formula One in 1993 as an independent manufacturer. The team competed across nearly three decades, achieving notable endurance racing success including a Le Mans victory, before being acquired by Audi in 2024 and subsequently ceasing independent operations in 2025 as one of the championship's longest-serving constructors.

team:McLaren.title: McLaren
team:McLaren.summary: McLaren Racing is a British Formula One outfit headquartered at its technology facility in Woking, operating as a subsidiary of the McLaren Group under Bahraini ownership. The team competes in the sport's premier championship class as an established competitor with deep roots in motorsport.

team:Mercedes.title: Mercedes-AMG Petronas F1 Team
team:Mercedes.summary: Mercedes-Benz has maintained a presence in Formula One since 1954 through team ownership and engine supply, with the current iteration operating as a Brackley-based squad competing under a German licence. The team became known as the "Silver Arrows" and underwent a significant ownership restructuring in 2022 when Ineos joined Mercedes-Benz Group and Toto Wolff as equal stakeholders.

team:Racing Bulls.title: Racing Bulls
team:Racing Bulls.summary: Visa Cash App Racing Bulls is an Italian Formula One team and constructor that commenced competition in 2024 as part of Red Bull's two-team operation. Based in Faenza with a secondary facility in Milton Keynes, the squad operates as Red Bull's sister team alongside the primary Red Bull Racing outfit.

team:RB.title: Racing Bulls
team:RB.summary: Visa Cash App Racing Bulls is an Italian Formula One team and constructor that entered the championship in 2024 as the second team under Red Bull's ownership. Based in Faenza with operations in Milton Keynes, it operates alongside Red Bull Racing as part of the Austrian conglomerate's dual-team strategy in the sport.

team:Red Bull Racing.title: Red Bull Racing
team:Red Bull Racing.summary: Oracle Red Bull Racing, an Austrian-licensed team headquartered in England, emerged as a Formula One competitor in 2005 under the ownership of Red Bull GmbH. Led by Christian Horner throughout its first two decades, the team transitioned to Laurent Mekies as team principal in 2025, while remaining part of Red Bull's dual-team racing portfolio.

team:Williams.title: Williams Racing
team:Williams.summary: Williams Grand Prix Engineering, established in 1977 by Frank Williams and Patrick Head following Williams's earlier failed F1 venture, stands as a British constructor competing in Formula One. Currently operating as Atlassian Williams F1 Team for 2026, the squad operates from its Grove, Oxfordshire headquarters.
